summary: Iteration 6 - Phase 2 backend additions tested. Added 17 new tests for Stripe billing (plans/checkout/status), Admin auth + API keys (login/create/list/delete with X-Admin-Password), and Public v1 API (text-to-sign + dictionary protected by X-API-Key with daily limits). 16/17 new tests pass. All 60 prior tests still pass (no regressions). One critical failure: GET /api/billing/status/{session_id} returns 502 'No such checkout.session' for sessions just created via POST /api/billing/checkout — same key, same session_id, reproducible.

critical.issue: Returns 502 'No such checkout.session' for a session that was just successfully created by POST /api/billing/checkout (same STRIPE_API_KEY=sk_test_emergent, same session_id). Reproducible 100%. Likely an issue with the emergentintegrations StripeCheckout client when used with the sentinel key sk_test_emergent — sessions appear to be created on one Stripe account but retrieval is routed to another. This breaks the post-checkout success page flow on /precios?session_id=... which polls this endpoint to confirm payment.

action_items: Fix GET /api/billing/status/{session_id}: investigate why retrieving a session created with sk_test_emergent fails with 'No such checkout.session'. May require: (1) calling emergentintegrations StripeCheckout differently for retrieval, (2) using a real sk_test_... key in /app/backend/.env, or (3) using web_search to look up correct emergentintegrations Stripe checkout retrieval pattern. Without this fix, the success-page flow after Stripe payment will not work.
Consider also testing webhook delivery (/api/webhook/stripe) once status retrieval is fixed.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:881 — db.payment_transactions update only sets new status if not already paid (good — idempotent). But still calls Stripe even when tx is already paid; consider short-circuiting when tx.payment_status=='paid'.

server.py:982 — admin_create_key reads X-Admin-Password from FastAPI Header param `x_admin_password` (default '') OR request.headers fallback. The Header param is `x_admin_password: str = ''` (not declared with Header(...)), so FastAPI treats it as a query param. The fallback to request.headers does work, but cleaner to use `Header(None, alias='X-Admin-Password')`.

server.py:1024 — _check_api_key has TOCTOU race: usage_today read then incremented in non-atomic two-step. For concurrent requests at limit boundary, could allow a few extra calls past daily_limit. Not critical but could combine into a single conditional update.

server.py:1024 — usage_today is never reset to 0 at midnight. Documented daily_limit will become a permanent total limit. Needs a reset job or per-day bucket key (e.g., usage_by_day dict keyed by ISO date).
server.py:81 — ADMIN_PASSWORD defaults to 'change-me' if env missing — fine but worth logging a warning at startup.

success_rate.backend: 98.7% (75/76 ran, 1 failed; 1 skipped due to missing ffmpeg)

test_credentials: ADMIN_PASSWORD=signlanguage-admin-2026 (verified working). STRIPE_API_KEY=sk_test_emergent (creates sessions but cannot retrieve them).
seed_data_creation: Each run creates one TEST_apikey_<rand> in db.api_keys (deleted at end), one Stripe checkout session in db.payment_transactions (left as test data — has TEST_buyer@example.com email).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 6 added 17 tests after line 885 of /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py covering Phase 2 features. Use `pytest -k 'Iter6 or Billing or Admin or PublicV1'` to run only Phase 2. The failing test test_checkout_status_for_just_created_session reproduces the Stripe sentinel-key retrieval bug — leave it failing until main agent fixes the integration. usage_today counter does not reset daily — relevant for any 429 limit testing across multiple test runs (re-run will reuse same key only if not deleted). Tests assume daily_limit=5 and burn the quota in test_daily_limit_429 — order matters within TestPublicV1WithKey class.
rca of the issue: POST /api/billing/checkout calls StripeCheckout(api_key='sk_test_emergent', webhook_url=...).create_checkout_session(...) — returns a real Stripe test session with valid checkout.stripe.com URL. GET /api/billing/status/{sid} calls StripeCheckout(api_key='sk_test_emergent', webhook_url='').get_checkout_status(sid). Stripe responds 'No such checkout.session: cs_test_...'. Reproduction: curl POST /api/billing/checkout body {'package_id':'pro_monthly','origin_url':'https://example.com'} → 200 with session_id; immediately GET /api/billing/status/<that session_id> → 502. Mitigation options: (a) confirm with emergent-integration docs whether sk_test_emergent supports session retrieval; (b) switch to a real sk_test_... key in backend/.env; (c) check if StripeCheckout exposes an account-aware retrieval method requiring metadata or stripe_account header passed through.
